title: anethum graveolens var. sowa
intro: Anethum graveolens var. sowa, also known as the Ajwain plant, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family that is native to the Indian subcontinent. This plant is prized for its seeds, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their carminative and stomachic properties. The medicinal properties of the Ajwain plant include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and lower blood pressure. The seeds can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are believed to provide health benefits including relief from indigestion, bloating, and respiratory issues.
